notes: Staged LLM extraction directly from each source span. Consumes only the span text and declared source metadata; never the manual object set or expected_* labels. Review priority is assigned application-side by type. Prompt version: v0.

predictions.predicted_statement: New clients must pay the standard deposit before scheduling can be finalized, unless an approved exception applies.
Emergency priority incidents bypass the requirement for operations lead approval for after‑hours scheduling.
Dispatch may treat active water damage, loss of heat, or other safety issues as emergencies and bypass the normal after‑hours approval step.
VIP maintenance clients may bypass the standard deposit when the dispatcher confirms the account and records the reason.
Before confirming a job, check if a documented exception applies and record the waiver reason in the job record.
Emergency dispatch must classify the incident and verify technician availability.
Long-term maintenance clients are identified by an active maintenance plan and at least one service visit in the past year.
The VIP client list comprises long‑term maintenance accounts that receive deposit flexibility and expedited handling.
Future emergency priority will only apply to active damage, safety hazards, or service outages that affect core habitability.

predictions.predicted_title: Notify field lead when tech unavailable
predictions.predicted_statement: The dispatcher must notify the field lead if no qualified technician is available for a same‑day emergency request.
Deposit exceptions must be recorded in the job record prior to confirming scheduling.
If an emergency request lacks a qualified technician, field lead reassignment approval must be obtained before same‑day service can be promised.
VIP deposit waivers apply only to accounts that have an active maintenance plan, effective immediately.
During after-hours intake, verify that the issue qualifies as an emergency before seeking operations lead approval.
After‑hours calls require operations lead approval unless the outage affects core habitability.
